en_body:
Mr. Trần Cao Lĩnh quoted from a letter one of his friends wrote to his son:
 During an exhibition of paintings and photographs of the homeland, Uncle Trần Cao Lĩnh whispered to Father: "Perhaps in this strange land there is a lack of Culture, my friend. Because people 'lack a smile.'
 "Văn" is beauty, "Hóa" is becoming. All activities, thoughts, and actions that make human life fresher and more beautiful are Culture.
 The thought of Culture begins germinating within you while you are still a newborn-red embryo in your mother's womb. Hearing that Mother had conceived, both Father and Mother rejoiced. Mother especially prayed day and night to bear a child beautiful and good. That prayer influences the embryo, steeping into your blood while heart and mind are only beginning to form. That prayer for "beauty and goodness" is the seed of Culture. Parents sow the seed, but whether the tree of Culture grows beautiful and green depends upon your tending it now and afterward.
 Beautiful thought is the Leaf of Culture, beautiful action is the Flower of Culture; loving-kindness is the Sap of Culture. You may examine the tree of Culture within yourself each night before sleeping, by asking: What beauty have I thought? What beauty have I done? If the answer is none, the tree of Culture has stopped growing. Whenever you have an ugly thought, one leaf of Culture begins to wither, both petal and calyx. The arrogance, the selfishness of each individual is the acid that kills the tree of Culture their parents planted. For it is the source of ugly thought, of ugly action.
 Human thought and action issue in two directions: for oneself and for others. For oneself is selfishness, is becoming-ugly; for others is selflessness, is Culture. Therefore Father has told you many times: "Nurtured in Freedom, you may think and act whatever and however you wish, provided it is not selfish and does not betray your own conscience."*
 The flowers in my garden are poems excavating to the ultimate meaning of the purpose of the long Resistance upon the road of Spiritual Revolution. I know well my compatriots are tormented within the red nightmare; I know well that through more than 32 years I have struggled and striven with them, and our happiness is the objective of the inner struggle: yet painful Truth remains Truth, while within me the flower-garden I tirelessly cultivate, hoping to offer them a dawn of consciousness, is also very real. The flowers of Culture bloom in abundance to remind and urge the mind imprisoned by delusion and despair: if the road we choose to enter is difficult, thorned, and perilous: then the purpose and ultimate end of that choosing is precisely the labor of transforming the human realm into gardens of Culture within each of us, where flower-buds breathe the fragrance of humane Human-Dignity. To me this Truth alone is the enduring Truth. Though I neither hear nor see the tall tree falling in the deep forest, I do not arrogantly declare that because I know nothing of a Truth, that Truth does not Exist. Moreover, if I cannot recognize Truth-Goodness-Beauty as the ultimate end of my actions, what strength of mind and will could ever help me become a mature person useful to human society?

Please, One Rose (2)
 Mr. Trần Cao Lĩnh quoted from a letter one of his friends wrote to his son:

 The thought of Culture begins germinating within you while you are still a newborn-red embryo in your mother's womb. Hearing that Mother had conceived, both Father and Mother rejoiced. Mother especially prayed day and night to bear a child beautiful and good. That prayer influences the embryo, steeping into your blood while heart and mind are only beginning to form.  The flowers in my garden are poems excavating to the ultimate meaning of the purpose of the long Resistance upon the road of Spiritual Revolution. I know well my compatriots are tormented within the red nightmare; I know well that through more than 32 years I have struggled and striven with them, and our happiness is the objective of the inner struggle: yet painful Truth remains Truth, while within me the flower-garden I tirelessly cultivate, hoping to offer them a dawn of consciousness, is also very real. note: “Văn hóa,” the Vietnamese word for Culture, is unfolded etymologically as beauty or refinement (“văn”) transformed into being (“hóa”).
